message.content:
You are the ADVERSARIAL GATE for an autonomous research-report system. Your job is to REFUTE, not to agree. A prior gate of yours already saved this project by refuting a confident-but-wrong diagnosis; do that again if it is warranted. Read the real code and real artifacts — do not reason from my summary alone.

REPO: /home/polaris/wt/flywheel (branch flywheel-v1). Python. Read-only: DO NOT EDIT ANY FILE.

## CONTEXT
This system writes literature reviews. Known quality gap: our reports are ~4k words; frontier competitors (ChatGPT/Gemini Deep Research) produce 10-22k. We just landed a topic judge (Rank5) that deletes genuinely off-topic rows from the evidence pool (507/944 deleted, validated: 97% row-level verdict stability across independently shuffled passes; zero seminal papers lost). The pool went 944 -> ~437 rows. The report got shorter but truer. So the remaining honest way to regain LENGTH is FRESH ON-TOPIC EVIDENCE (more retrieval), not padding.

The outline agent (src/polaris_graph/outline/outline_agent.py) has a `search_more_evidence` tool = live gap-fill retrieval. Budget: max 24 turns (PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS), one fetch per turn, 900s wall.

## MY DIAGNOSIS (attack it)
The gap-fill loop is driven by `_run_checklist` (outline_agent.py ~1277-1542). It asks the LLM for deficiencies of three kinds: `coverage | density | numeric_rows`. Each returned line is `<section> :: <aspect> :: <kind> :: <verbatim quote from QUESTION>`.

CLAIM 1 (structural): at line ~1454 the code runs `_quote_is_grounded(quote, question_norm)` on EVERY line and drops it as "ungrounded" if the quote is not a literal substring (>= _MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS words) of the RESEARCH QUESTION. This runs BEFORE `kind` is parsed (line ~1458). Therefore a DENSITY deficiency ("this section is backed by too few sources") must also produce a verbatim quote from the question naming the missing facet — but thinness of a section is not a property the question's wording ever names. So the density arm is structurally near-unfireable.

CLAIM 2 (empirical): across every log the system has ever written (logs/*.log), the recorded gap kinds are 28 `coverage`, 1 `numeric_rows`, and ZERO `density`. The density arm has never fired once.

CLAIM 3 (consequence): retrieval therefore stops as soon as every facet LITERALLY NAMED in the question has some section, no matter how thin. Evidence: the Rank5 armed run (logs/rank5_armed2.log) logged `checklist[seed] ran: NONE (no grounded deficiencies)` and `finish_outline ACCEPTED at turn 8: pending=0, budget_remains=True` — 0 gap-fill fetches, 16 turns of retrieval budget unspent — while its outline (outputs/rank5_deleted_outline.json) has sections with 6, 9, and 17 rows next to sections with 78 and 51. The 3-sentence question is in /home/polaris/polaris_project/task72_prompt.txt. Note the dirty-pool control run (logs/rank4_judged.log) DID find gaps (`checklist[seed] named 2 gap(s)`) and fetched 3 times.

## MY PROPOSED FIX (Rank6) — attack this hardest
1. Parse `kind` BEFORE the grounding gate (pure reorder of the existing lines).
2. kind=`coverage`: keep the question-quote gate EXACTLY as today. Unchanged. It is a working anti-invention precision gate (it correctly kills hallucinated "Summary Table" sections — see the `dropped N ungrounded line(s)` log lines).
3. kind=`density`: change the grounding ANCHOR (not remove it). A density line must quote verbatim from the NAMED SECTION'S OWN title+focus (which is itself already question-derived and already an approved part of the outline) rather than from the question. Rationale: a density gap by definition asks for MORE OF AN ALREADY-APPROVED TOPIC, so it cannot introduce new subject matter; the anti-invention property is preserved, just anchored to the right text.
4. Add a MECHANICAL precision guard the prompt currently only states in prose: accept a density line only if the named section's actual ev_id count is genuinely low relative to the outline (e.g. below median / below a floor), so the model cannot claim the 78-row section is thin.
5. Bound the blast radius: cap density todos per checklist round (K thinnest sections); coverage gaps served before density gaps; existing PG_OUTLINE_GAP_RETRIES_PER_ASPECT=2 -> UNFILLED still applies. New lever ships behind a DEFAULT-OFF env kill-switch (PG_OUTLINE_DENSITY_GAPS) for a clean A/B.

SAFETY ARGUMENT I AM RELYING ON (test it): retrieving more rows is now SAFE in a way it wasn't before, because gap-fill rows are folded in through `src/polaris_graph/outline/_fold_in.py` which applies the SAME content-integrity junk detector AND the SAME (now-armed, now-validated) topic judge as the corpus gate. So fresh alien/chrome rows get screened at fold-in.

## YOUR TASK
Verify every claim against the actual code and artifacts. Then adversarially attack:
(a) Is CLAIM 1 actually true, or is there some other path by which a density gap could fire (a different producer, a fallback, a remap, _screen_status_lines behavior)? Is the density arm truly dead, or merely rare? Is `numeric_rows`'s single firing a counterexample that breaks my reasoning?
(b) Is my causal story for `checklist[seed] ran: NONE` right? Could the NONE instead be caused by something else entirely (seed outline shape, a different prompt path, the LLM's Rule-5 anti-redundancy nudge, an exception, a screen)? What OTHER explanation fits the evidence better? Note the dirty run found gaps and the clean run didn't — explain that under YOUR best theory, not mine.
(c) Will my fix actually produce DEPTH, or will it produce a retrieval treadmill / junk influx / turn starvation / infinite thin-section loop? What breaks?
(d) Is the section-title+focus anchor a real anti-invention gate, or is it trivially gameable (the focus text is model-generated — is it a weaker gate than I claim, and does that matter)?
(e) Is the "_fold_in screens fetched rows" safety argument TRUE? Read _fold_in.py and verify the junk + topic screens actually run on gap-fill rows, with what defaults, and whether they can be silently inert (this project has a history of gates that were dead code or no-ops).
(f) What is the SINGLE highest-value thing I am missing? Is there a better lever for the 4k-vs-20k depth gap than the density arm? Be concrete. Consider: is the outline agent even the binding constraint on final word count, or is the COMPOSE/generation stage the real ceiling (i.e. would more evidence rows even become more words)? That last question may be the most important one — check how section word budgets are actually determined, because if compose caps words independently of evidence, my whole Rank6 is worthless and you must tell me so.

Deliver: VERDICT (PASS / PASS-WITH-FIX / REFUTED) + numbered findings, each with file:line and quoted code or quoted artifact/log evidence. Explicitly list which of my claims are WRONG. Prioritize (f) — if there is a bigger lever, name it with evidence.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1547	        """Deterministic: empty comparison / zero datapoints / empty SQL / <2 studies -> auto
1548	        todo. This is a ROUTING signal from a mechanical check (empty result), not a quality
1549	        verdict about the corpus (§-1.1 — the mechanical check here fires a SEARCH action, it
1550	        never states a quality number)."""

270	        "PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD", _SECTION_TITLE_FUZZY_JACCARD_DEFAULT,
271	    )

274	def _section_remap_jaccard_threshold() -> float:
275	    """Threshold for remapping a facet to the EXISTING section whose own topic (title+focus)
276	    it most overlaps, when no section in the outline is even fuzzily the SAME section — low
277	    bar by design: under a locked required-structure deliverable, a genuine content facet
278	    must live in SOME existing section, never invent a new one, and 'weight don't filter'
279	    (§-1.3) means routing beats discarding whenever there is ANY real signal."""
280	    return _env_float("PG_OUTLINE_SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD", _SECTION_REMAP_JACCARD_DEFAULT)
281	
282	
283	# iter-2 P0-1 fix: the checklist's anti-invention grounding gate. A candidate deficiency line
284	# must carry a verbatim quote from the research question; these two helpers are the mechanical
285	# (never-LLM) check that the quote is real, not a paraphrase or a hallucinated snippet.
286	_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS = 2
287	
288	
289	_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS = "\"'‘’“”‹›«»`"
290	
291	
292	def _normalize_for_quote_check(text: str) -> str:
293	    import re as _re
294	    stripped = str(text or "").strip().strip(_QUOTE_WRAP_CHARS).strip()
295	    return _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", stripped.lower())
296	
297	
298	def _quote_is_grounded(quote: str, question_norm: str) -> bool:
299	    """True iff ``quote`` (whitespace/case-normalized, and with any wrapping quote-mark
300	    PUNCTUATION the model added around its own answer stripped — iter-4 robustness fix: a
301	    model reply like the literal three characters ``"foo bar"`` around an otherwise-correct
302	    verbatim excerpt must not be mechanically rejected just because those wrapping marks are
303	    not themselves part of the source text) is a literal substring of the (already-normalized)
304	    research question AND carries at least ``_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS`` words (a bare
305	    one-word quote like the subject's name is not a specific-enough justification for a
306	    claimed missing FACET)."""
307	    q = _normalize_for_quote_check(quote)
308	    if not q or len(q.split()) < _MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS:
309	        return False
310	    return q in question_norm

313	@dataclass
314	class GapTodo:
315	    section: str
316	    aspect: str
317	    needed_kind: str = "coverage"   # "coverage" | "numeric_rows" | "density"
318	    status: str = "PENDING"          # PENDING | IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETE | UNFILLED
319	    attempts: int = 0
320	    source: str = "checklist"        # checklist | tool_failure | agent
321	    disclosure: str = ""
322	
323	    def key(self) -> tuple[str, str]:
324	        return (str(self.section), str(self.aspect))
325	
326	
327	class GapLedger:
328	    """Dedup-by-(section,aspect) EXACT match, falling back to same-section token-overlap
329	    (Jaccard >= threshold) paraphrase collapse; per-aspect retry cap; UNFILLED + disclosed
330	    on exhaustion.
331	
332	    §-1.3.1 / §-1.1: this ledger is a ROUTING signal (what to search next), never a quality
333	    verdict about the corpus — it names todos, it does not score the pipeline's own output.
334	    """
335	
336	    def __init__(self, max_retries_per_aspect: Optional[int] = None):
337	        self._todos: dict[tuple[str, str], GapTodo] = {}
338	        self._max_retries = (
339	            max_retries_per_aspect
340	            if max_retries_per_aspect is not None
341	            else _gap_retries_per_aspect()
342	        )
343	
344	    def _find_paraphrase(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
345	        """Same-section, token-overlap match against an EXISTING todo (any status). Iter-2
346	        P1-1: collapses re-worded checklist gaps onto the one todo already tracking that
347	        aspect so the per-aspect retry cap actually bites."""
348	        canon_new = _canon_tokens(aspect)
349	        if not canon_new:
350	            return None
351	        threshold = _aspect_dedup_threshold()
352	        best: Optional[GapTodo] = None
353	        best_score = 0.0
354	        for (sec2, asp2), todo in self._todos.items():
355	            if sec2 != section:
356	                continue
357	            score = _jaccard(canon_new, _canon_tokens(asp2))
358	            if score >= threshold and score > best_score:
359	                best, best_score = todo, score
360	        return best
361	
362	    def add(
363	        self, section: str, aspect: str,
364	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
365	    ) -> GapTodo:
366	        section = str(section or "").strip()
367	        aspect = str(aspect or "").strip()
368	        key = (section, aspect)
369	        existing = self._todos.get(key)
370	        if existing is not None:
371	            return existing
372	        paraphrase = self._find_paraphrase(section, aspect)
373	        if paraphrase is not None:
374	            return paraphrase
375	        todo = GapTodo(
376	            section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source,
377	        )
378	        self._todos[key] = todo
379	        return todo
380	
381	    def add_unfillable(
382	        self, section: str, aspect: str, reason: str,
383	        needed_kind: str = "coverage", source: str = "checklist",
384	    ) -> GapTodo:
385	        """Iter-3 P0 fix (real-corpus degeneration — 'Summary Table' gap re-fired ~10x over
386	        945s while every fetched row stayed orphaned in the pool): a gap whose named section
387	        can NEVER be routed to retrieval — it does not match any current outline title, and
388	        under a locked required-structure deliverable there is no way to add a new section for
389	        it — is a STRUCTURAL limitation, not a content gap. Recording it UNFILLED immediately
390	        (never PENDING) means ``next_pending()`` never selects it and the decide-loop never
391	        fires ``search_more_evidence`` for it, so the loop cannot burn its turn/wall budget
392	        re-fetching the same unhomeable aspect under a slightly different checklist wording
393	        each round. Still disclosed (§-1.3 — no silent drop), just never retried."""
394	        todo = self.add(section=section, aspect=aspect, needed_kind=needed_kind, source=source)
395	        if todo.status not in ("UNFILLED", "COMPLETE"):
396	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
397	            todo.disclosure = reason
398	        return todo
399	
400	    def get(self, section: str, aspect: str) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
401	        return self._todos.get((str(section or "").strip(), str(aspect or "").strip()))
402	
403	    def next_pending(self) -> Optional[GapTodo]:
404	        for todo in self._todos.values():
405	            if todo.status == "PENDING":
406	                return todo
407	        return None
408	
409	    def mark_in_progress(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
410	        todo.status = "IN_PROGRESS"
411	        todo.attempts += 1
412	
413	    def mark_complete(self, todo: GapTodo) -> None:
414	        todo.status = "COMPLETE"
415	
416	    def mark_retry_or_unfilled(self, todo: GapTodo, reason: str) -> None:
417	        """Per-aspect retry: PENDING again while attempts < cap; UNFILLED + disclosed at cap."""
418	        if todo.attempts >= self._max_retries:
419	            todo.status = "UNFILLED"
420	            todo.disclosure = reason
421	        else:
422	            todo.status = "PENDING"
423	
424	    @property
425	    def pending_count(self) -> int:
426	        return sum(1 for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "PENDING")
427	
428	    @property
429	    def unfilled(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
430	        return [t for t in self._todos.values() if t.status == "UNFILLED"]
431	
432	    @property
433	    def all_todos(self) -> list[GapTodo]:
434	        return list(self._todos.values())
435	
436	    def as_list(self) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
437	        return [dataclasses.asdict(t) for t in self._todos.values()]
438	
439	    def summary_for_llm(self) -> str:
440	        if not self._todos:
441	            return "  (empty — no gaps identified yet)"
442	        lines = []
443	        for t in self._todos.values():
444	            lines.append(
445	                f"  [{t.status}] section={t.section!r} aspect={t.aspect!r} "
446	                f"kind={t.needed_kind} attempts={t.attempts}/{self._max_retries} source={t.source}"
447	            )
448	        return "\n".join(lines)

1	"""
2	Multi-section generator — HONEST-REBUILD Gap-4.
3	
4	Three-stage architecture that produces 1500-3000-word reports while
5	keeping per-section provenance tightness:
6	
7	  1. OUTLINE stage  (1 LLM call, ~500 tokens)
8	     DeepSeek reads all evidence and emits a JSON section plan:
9	       [{"title": "Efficacy", "focus": "...", "ev_ids": ["ev_001", ...]},
10	        {"title": "Safety", "focus": "...", "ev_ids": [...]},
11	        {"title": "Comparative", ...}]
12	     Sections constrained to a fixed allowed set so the model can't
13	     invent topics unsupported by evidence.
14	
15	  2. PER-SECTION GENERATION  (N parallel LLM calls, ~800 tokens each)
16	     Each section gets its own prompt with ONLY its evidence subset +
17	     focus statement. Generates 8-15 sentences with [ev_XXX] markers.
18	
19	  3. VERIFY + OPTIONAL REGEN  (deterministic + 0-N retry calls)
20	     Each section is strict_verified. If <50% sentences kept, the
21	     section is regenerated ONCE with a "tighter citations required"
22	     reminder. If regen still fails, the section is dropped (with a
23	     note in the report).
24	
25	  4. ASSEMBLY
26	     verified_sections + shared Methods + contradictions + Limitations
27	     + bibliography, concatenated.
28	
29	Cost estimate: ~$0.01-$0.02 per report (vs $0.0022 for single-call).
30	"""
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import asyncio
34	import contextvars
35	import difflib
36	import json
37	import logging
38	import os
39	import re
40	import time
41	from dataclasses import dataclass, field, replace
42	from typing import Any, Optional
43	
44	import httpx
45	
46	from src.polaris_graph.generator.live_deepseek_generator import (
47	    _DECIMAL_RE,
48	    _EV_MARKER_RE,
49	    _rewrite_draft_with_spans,
50	    build_prompt,
51	)
52	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
53	    resolve_provenance_to_citations,
54	    resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count,
55	    sanitize_evidence_text,
56	    strict_verify,
57	    wrap_evidence_for_prompt,
58	)
59	# I-arch-011 #1268 PR-c: per-basket verified-compose (the PRIMARY section-prose producer when
60	# PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE is on). Re-exported here so callers + the replay harness share one source.

505	# much of a facet's matched evidence can render REGARDLESS of how much verified evidence exists — a
506	# facet with 40 matched rows keeps only 30. That is a FILTER-AND-CAP the §-1.3 DNA bans. When this
507	# flag is ON, the per-section cap TRACKS the section's full matched payload (the ``min(.., 30)``
508	# ceiling is dropped); the SACRED per-facet reserved set is still never truncated, and the default
509	# char-budget path (I-arch-005) is unchanged (it already dissolves the row cap into a 120K-char
510	# compute bound). Read at CALL time (monkeypatch-testable). Default-OFF => the exact legacy row-cap
511	# ceiling => byte-identical.
512	def _ev_budget_tracks_payload() -> bool:
513	    """True iff PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD removes the PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row-cap CEILING so a
514	    section's evidence budget tracks its full matched payload (F2 cap-removal). Default-OFF."""
515	    return os.getenv("PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD", "0").strip().lower() not in (
516	        "", "0", "false", "off", "no",
517	    )
518	
519	
520	# ── RACE-FLOOR lever 2 (#1344 R2): WRITER-MENU top-N ev-density cap ───────────────────────────────
521	# THE DRAG (drb_72 R2 render, MEASURED): with PG_ROUTE_ALL_BASKETS + PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD ON,
522	# route_all/facet-route APPEND ~orphan tails so each body section's ``ev_ids`` balloons to 52-103 rows
523	# (vs the deep step3 run's ~15/section). The abstractive writer is then prompted over that crammed
524	# menu and strict_verify DROPS ~65% of what it writes (post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.35) -> THIN prose
525	# (2141 body words vs step3's 3230) and 102 dropped sentences. This cap FOCUSES the WRITER's PROMPT
526	# menu to the top-N highest-ranked rows (``section.ev_ids`` is already ranked: reserved-facet +
527	# authority-ordered head first, route_all/facet-route orphans appended to the TAIL), so the writer
528	# composes deep, well-grounded prose over the primaries exactly like the step3 draw.
529	#
530	# §-1.3 / FAITHFULNESS (why this is provably SAFE — not a source drop, not a verify weakening):
531	#   * It caps ONLY ``ev_subset`` — the rows the WRITER is PROMPTED with. It does NOT touch
532	#     ``section.ev_ids`` (bibliography + credibility disclosure are built from that, so every routed
533	#     row STAYS cited-eligible + disclosed) and does NOT touch ``evidence_pool``.
534	#   * strict_verify / ``_rewrite_draft_with_spans`` gate every emitted sentence against the FULL
535	#     ``evidence_pool`` (NOT ``ev_subset``) — so a capped-out row can still GROUND a sentence the
536	#     writer happened to write, and no sentence is ever admitted that the full pool cannot verify.
537	#     The frozen faithfulness engine (strict_verify / NLI / [#calc] / fold-in) is UNTOUCHED.
538	#   * The selection is DETERMINISTIC (head-N of the already-ranked list) and DISCLOSED (LOUD
539	#     activation log naming the withheld count per section).
540	# Default-OFF (``PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION`` unset / <=0) => ``ev_subset`` is the full assigned
541	# set => byte-identical legacy render.
542	def _writer_topn_ev_per_section() -> int:
543	    """Top-N cap on the per-section WRITER evidence menu (``ev_subset``). ``PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION``
544	    read at call time (monkeypatch-testable). <=0 / non-integer => 0 => the cap is OFF (byte-identical:
545	    the writer sees every assigned row)."""
546	    try:
547	        v = int(os.getenv("PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION", "0").strip())
548	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
549	        return 0
550	    return v if v > 0 else 0
551	
552	
553	def _apply_writer_menu_cap(
554	    ev_subset: list, *, section_title: str = "", total_assigned: int = 0
555	) -> list:
556	    """Return the top-N head of ``ev_subset`` (the WRITER prompt menu) per
557	    ``_writer_topn_ev_per_section()``. Returns a NEW list (never mutates the input, so the caller's
558	    ``section.ev_ids`` / ``evidence_pool`` are never touched). Cap<=0 or a shorter menu => the input
559	    list unchanged (byte-identical). Discloses the withheld tail LOUD when it bites."""
560	    cap = _writer_topn_ev_per_section()
561	    if cap <= 0 or len(ev_subset) <= cap:
562	        return ev_subset
563	    withheld = len(ev_subset) - cap
564	    logger.info(
565	        "[multi_section] %s RACE-FLOOR writer-menu top-N cap: prompting writer with top %d/%d "
566	        "assigned row(s); %d tail row(s) WITHHELD FROM THE WRITER PROMPT ONLY (kept in "
567	        "evidence_pool + bibliography + credibility disclosure; strict_verify still gates every "
568	        "rendered sentence against the FULL pool — faithfulness engine untouched)",
569	        section_title, cap, total_assigned or len(ev_subset), withheld,
570	    )
571	    return ev_subset[:cap]
572	
573	
574	# I-arch-002 (#1246) P-W4gen: per-section serialized CHARACTER budget that REPLACES the
575	# PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION row cap under the redesign flag. Read at CALL time. Default is a
576	# generous slice of the 1M-context generator's window (~120K chars ≈ a large fraction of
577	# the context) so a section keeps every assigned row whose serialized
578	# statement+direct_quote fits — never a row count. The faithfulness engine re-verifies
579	# every emitted sentence regardless of how many rows reach the prompt.

1	"""THE shared fold-in seam for the agentic outliner (factored 2026-07-11).
2	
3	Every path that brings EXTERNAL fetched content into ``workspace.ev_store`` — ``search_more_evidence``
4	today, ``fetch_url`` next — MUST re-enter through this ONE seam so the faithfulness engine stays the
5	only hard gate and the id space can never collide. Factored out of ``outline_agent.py`` (was
6	``_offset_renumber`` :629 + ``_stamp_and_delete`` :802) so both tools share the identical unit
7	instead of copy-pasting it.
8	
9	The seam, in order (design §):
10	  1. URL-dedup vs. the rows already in ``ev_store`` (never re-fold a page we already hold).
11	  2. ``_offset_renumber`` — assign ``ev_{offset+i}`` ids and HARD fail-loud if any new id collides
12	     with an existing id (a collision is a programming bug in the offset math, not a data
13	     condition — LAW II: never swallow).
14	  3. ``_stamp_and_delete`` — S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome delete (pure) + topic-judge
15	     off-topic delete (LLM, fail-open). §-1.3.1 junk carve-out only; the rest of
16	     WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE is untouched downstream.
17	  4. Insert the survivors into ``ev_store``.
18	
19	No number and no citation is ever invented here; this seam only renumbers, dedups, and deletes
20	junk. Computed numbers take the SEPARATE verified ``[#calc:]`` lane (outline/verified_compute.py).
21	"""
22	from __future__ import annotations
23	
24	import logging
25	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
26	from typing import Any
27	
28	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
29	
30	
31	def _offset_renumber(
32	    new_rows: list[dict[str, Any]], offset: int, existing_ids: set[str],
33	) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
34	    """THE sharp seam (design §): renumber ``new_rows`` to ``ev_{offset+i}`` and HARD
35	    fail-loud assert the new id set never intersects ``existing_ids``. A collision here is a
36	    programming bug in the offset computation, not a data condition — it must never be
37	    swallowed (LAW II: fail loudly, never silently degrade)."""
38	    renumbered: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
39	    new_ids: set[str] = set()
40	    for i, row in enumerate(new_rows):
41	        new_id = f"ev_{offset + i:03d}"
42	        if new_id in existing_ids or new_id in new_ids:
43	            raise AssertionError(
44	                f"outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED: {new_id!r} already present "
45	                f"(existing_ids has it: {new_id in existing_ids}; batch dup: {new_id in new_ids}). "
46	                "This must never happen — the offset computation has a bug."
47	            )
48	        new_ids.add(new_id)
49	        copied = dict(row)
50	        copied["evidence_id"] = new_id
51	        renumbered.append(copied)
52	    assert new_ids.isdisjoint(existing_ids), (
53	        "outline_agent id-collision seam FAILED post-hoc: new ids intersect existing ids "
54	        f"({sorted(new_ids & existing_ids)})"
55	    )
56	    return renumbered
57	
58	
59	async def _stamp_and_delete(
60	    rows: list[dict[str, Any]], research_question: str, agent_model: str,
61	) -> tuple[list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]], list[dict[str, Any]]]:
62	    """S2 stamp pass: content-integrity chrome detection (pure, no LLM) THEN semantic
63	    topic-judge off-topic screen (LLM, fail-open). Returns (kept, deleted_chrome,
64	    deleted_offtopic). §-1.3.1: only genuine junk/off-topic is deleted here — the rest of
65	    the WEIGHT-AND-CONSOLIDATE DNA (tier/credibility weighting) is untouched downstream."""
66	    if not rows:
67	        return [], [], []
68	
69	    from src.tools.access_bypass import detect_content_integrity_junk  # noqa: PLC0415
70	
71	    survivors: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
72	    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
73	    for row in rows:
74	        body = max(
75	            (
76	                str(row.get(k) or "") for k in (
77	                    "fetched_body", "full_text", "content", "extracted_text",
78	                    "raw_content", "raw_text", "page_text", "direct_quote",
79	                    "statement", "source_text", "body", "text",
80	                )
81	            ),
82	            key=len, default="",
83	        )
84	        url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
85	        title = str(row.get("title") or row.get("source_title") or "")
86	        try:
87	            is_junk, cls = detect_content_integrity_junk(body, url, title)
88	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — fail-open, never delete on a predicate bug
89	            logger.warning("[outline_agent] content-integrity check errored (fail-open): %s", exc)
90	            is_junk, cls = False, ""
91	        if is_junk:
92	            copied = dict(row)
93	            copied["deletion_reason"] = f"content_integrity_junk:{cls}"
94	            deleted_chrome.append(copied)
95	        else:
96	            survivors.append(row)
97	
98	    if not survivors:
99	        return [], deleted_chrome, []
100	
101	    try:
102	        # Lazy imports from outline_agent avoid a module-load import cycle (outline_agent imports
103	        # THIS module at top). By call time outline_agent is fully initialized.
104	        from src.polaris_graph.outline.outline_agent import (  # noqa: PLC0415
105	            _env_int, _sync_llm_bridge,
106	        )
107	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.topic_relevance_gate import (  # noqa: PLC0415
108	            classify_topic_relevance, topic_gate_enabled,
109	        )
110	        if not topic_gate_enabled():
111	            return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
112	        llm_callable = _sync_llm_bridge(
113	            agent_model, _env_int("PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS", 1200),
114	        )
115	        import asyncio  # noqa: PLC0415
116	        topic_result = await asyncio.to_thread(
117	            classify_topic_relevance, survivors, research_question, llm_callable,
118	        )
119	        kept = list(getattr(topic_result, "kept_rows", None) or survivors)
120	        dropped = list(getattr(topic_result, "dropped_rows", None) or [])
121	        deleted_offtopic = []
122	        for row in dropped:
123	            copied = dict(row)
124	            copied["deletion_reason"] = "confirmed_offtopic_subject"
125	            deleted_offtopic.append(copied)
126	        return kept, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic
127	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — topic judge is fail-open by design
128	        logger.warning("[outline_agent] topic judge skipped (fail-open): %s", exc)
129	        return survivors, deleted_chrome, []
130	
131	
132	@dataclass
133	class FoldInResult:
134	    """The outcome of one fold-in of external fetched rows through the shared seam."""
135	
136	    kept_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
137	    n_url_dup: int = 0
138	    deleted_chrome: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
139	    deleted_offtopic: list[dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=list)
140	
141	    @property
142	    def n_kept(self) -> int:
143	        return len(self.kept_rows)
144	
145	    @property
146	    def n_deleted(self) -> int:
147	        return len(self.deleted_chrome) + len(self.deleted_offtopic)
148	
149	
150	async def fold_in_fetched_rows(
151	    workspace: Any,
152	    fetched_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
153	    *,
154	    research_question: str,
155	    agent_model: str,
156	    insert: bool = True,
157	) -> FoldInResult:
158	    """The reusable fold-in unit: url-dedup -> offset-renumber (hard id-collision assert) ->
159	    S2 stamp/delete -> insert survivors into ``workspace.ev_store``.
160	
161	    Shared by ``search_more_evidence`` and (next) ``fetch_url`` so a second content path can NEVER
162	    diverge from the id-collision guard or the junk/off-topic screen. Returns a ``FoldInResult``
163	    carrying the survivors and per-stage counts for the caller's disclosure/markdown. ``insert``
164	    is always True in production; a caller may set it False to inspect the survivors without
165	    mutating the store.
166	    """
167	    existing_urls = workspace.existing_urls()
168	    deduped_rows = [
169	        r for r in fetched_rows
170	        if str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip() not in existing_urls
171	        or not str(r.get("source_url") or r.get("url") or "").strip()
172	    ]
173	    n_url_dup = len(fetched_rows) - len(deduped_rows)
174	
175	    offset = workspace.next_ev_offset()
176	    existing_ids = set(workspace.ev_store.keys())
177	    renumbered = _offset_renumber(deduped_rows, offset, existing_ids)
178	
179	    kept_rows, deleted_chrome, deleted_offtopic = await _stamp_and_delete(
180	        renumbered, research_question, agent_model,
181	    )
182	
183	    if insert:
184	        for row in kept_rows:
185	            workspace.ev_store[row["evidence_id"]] = row
186	
187	    return FoldInResult(
188	        kept_rows=kept_rows,
189	        n_url_dup=n_url_dup,
190	        deleted_chrome=deleted_chrome,
191	        deleted_offtopic=deleted_offtopic,
192	    )

def topic_gate_enabled() -> bool:
    """Kill-switch ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE`` (default ON — I-deepfix-001 DEFER-1).

    Default flipped ``"0"->"1"`` (2026-06-30): the gate is the SEMANTIC
    discriminator the cite-surface off-topic suppression keys on (a lexical floor

def classify_topic_relevance(
    sources: list[dict[str, Any]],
    research_question: str,
    llm_callable: Callable[[str], str],
    *,
    batch_size: int | None = None,
    primary_trial_anchors: list[str] | None = None,
    anchor_predicate: Callable[[dict[str, Any]], bool] | None = None,
) -> TopicGateResult:
    """Drop sources CONFIDENTLY classified OFF-topic for the research question.

    Pure + side-effect-free (apart from logging): the caller supplies the LLM
    via ``llm_callable(prompt: str) -> str`` so this is fully unit-testable
    with a stub. Marquee / required-entity anchors are EXEMPT (never dropped).
    FAIL-OPEN: any LLM exception, count mismatch, or unparseable verdict keeps
    the whole batch. Drops ONLY on an explicit confident OFF verdict.

    Args:
        sources: evidence rows (selection-stage, post floor + dedup).
        research_question: the user's raw question (the topic anchor).
        llm_callable: synchronous ``str -> str`` LLM interface.
        batch_size: sources per LLM call (default :func:`topic_batch_size`).
        primary_trial_anchors: named-trial anchors; a row matching one is
            exempt (handled via ``anchor_predicate`` when supplied).
        anchor_predicate: optional extra "is this a primary anchor" test
            (the orchestrator passes the selector's anchor matcher so the
            exemption is identical to the floor stage). Marquee detection is
            always applied in addition.

    Returns:
        TopicGateResult with kept/dropped rows + honest telemetry.
    """
    n_in = len(sources)
    if n_in == 0:
        return TopicGateResult(
            kept_rows=[], dropped_rows=[], n_in=0, n_kept=0,
            n_dropped_offtopic=0, notes=["topic_gate: empty pool"],
        )
    if not (research_question or "").strip():
        # Nothing to anchor on — FAIL-OPEN, keep everything.
        return TopicGateResult(
            kept_rows=list(sources), dropped_rows=[], n_in=n_in,
            n_kept=n_in, n_dropped_offtopic=0,
            notes=["topic_gate: empty research_question — fail-open"],
        )

    size = batch_size if (batch_size and batch_size > 0) else topic_batch_size()

    def _is_exempt(row: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
        if _row_is_marquee_anchor(row):
            return True
        if anchor_predicate is not None:
            try:
                return bool(anchor_predicate(row))
            except Exception:
                return False
        return False

    # Partition exempt rows out — they bypass classification entirely.
    exempt_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    # hard-filter" as a BANNED anti-pattern; the ONLY hard gate is the
    # faithfulness engine): a confident-OFF source is, BY DEFAULT, KEPT in the
    # pool and DEMOTED (disclosed off-topic via the ``topic_offtopic_demoted``
    # sidecar), NEVER hard-dropped. Topicality is a WEIGHT — the source still
    # flows to composition where the UNCHANGED strict_verify / NLI / 4-role
    # engine is the only gate. The legacy hard-drop is preserved behind
    # ``PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP`` (audit / reversal). Order is unchanged in
    # BOTH modes (no tail-partition), so a demoted row stays best-first-ranked.
    hard_drop = topic_gate_hard_drop_enabled()
    rescue_on_stamp = os.environ.get("PG_TOPIC_GATE_RESCUE_ON_STAMP", "1").strip().lower() not in (
        "0", "false", "no", "off",
    )
    # I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3: split the OFF verdict into OFF_ASPECT (demote-keep) vs
    # OFF_SUBJECT (the only deletable OFF). Default ON; OFF = byte-identical legacy prompt
    # + two-verdict parser + no ``topic_off_subject`` sidecar.
    split = topic_gate_subject_aspect_split_enabled()
    offtopic_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    offtopic_titles: list[str] = []
    ontopic_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []  # I-deepfix-001: confident-ON, for the rescue False-stamp
    # I-deepfix-003 Fix 3: confident-OFF_SUBJECT rows (a subset of offtopic_rows) — the
    # ONLY rows that receive the deletable ``topic_off_subject=True`` sidecar. Empty
    # unless the subject/aspect split is enabled.
    offsubject_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    # I-deepfix-003 gate-fix (Codex P1): confident-OFF_ASPECT rows (same entity, wrong
    # aspect — demote-KEEP, NEVER deletable). Tracked so the deletable ``topic_off_subject``
    # sidecar can be CLEARED to False on them (demote path below) — a STALE True baked into
    # corpus_snapshot by an earlier run must NEVER survive on a row THIS run re-verdicts
    # OFF_ASPECT (it would be misread as a fresh OFF_SUBJECT and deleted). Empty unless the
    # subject/aspect split is enabled.
    offaspect_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    for start in range(0, len(judged_rows), size):
        end = min(start + size, len(judged_rows))
        batch_rows = judged_rows[start:end]
        batch_meta = judged_meta[start:end]
        batch = [
            (local_idx, batch_meta[local_idx][0], batch_meta[local_idx][1])
            for local_idx in range(len(batch_rows))
        ]
        expected = [b[0] for b in batch]
        prompt = _build_batch_prompt(
            research_question, batch, subject_aspect_split=split,
        )
        try:
            raw = llm_callable(prompt)
        except Exception as exc:  # FAIL-OPEN on any LLM error -> keep batch.
            _LOGGER.warning(
                "[scope] topic_gate batch LLM error — fail-open, keeping "
                "%d sources: %s", len(batch_rows), str(exc)[:200],
            )
            continue
        if split:
            verdicts = _parse_batch_verdicts_split(raw, expected)
        else:
            verdicts = _parse_batch_verdicts(raw, expected)
        if verdicts is None:  # FAIL-OPEN on count mismatch / unparseable.
            _LOGGER.warning(
                "[scope] topic_gate batch unparseable / count mismatch — "
                "fail-open, keeping %d sources", len(batch_rows),
            )
            continue
        for local_idx, row in enumerate(batch_rows):
            v = verdicts.get(local_idx)
            if split:
                # Three-verdict split. Both OFF kinds are DEMOTED (weight); only
                # OFF_SUBJECT additionally carries the deletable sidecar below.
                if v == "OFF_SUBJECT":
                    offtopic_rows.append(row)
                    offtopic_titles.append(batch_meta[local_idx][0] or "(no title)")
                    offsubject_rows.append(row)
                elif v == "OFF_ASPECT":
                    offtopic_rows.append(row)
                    offtopic_titles.append(batch_meta[local_idx][0] or "(no title)")
                    offaspect_rows.append(row)
                elif v == "ON":
                    ontopic_rows.append(row)

                # Stamping False on a confident-ON verdict lets a fixed-prompt re-judge un-bury the

                # (_is_confirmed_offtopic keys on `is True`; is_topic_unjudged on `is not None`) already
                # handles False. Gated PG_TOPIC_GATE_RESCUE_ON_STAMP (default ON); OFF = byte-identical.
                ontopic_rows.append(row)

    if hard_drop:
        # LEGACY (explicit opt-in): hard-drop the confident-OFF set.
        _off_ids = {id(r) for r in offtopic_rows}
        kept_rows = [r for r in sources if id(r) not in _off_ids]
        dropped_rows, dropped_titles = offtopic_rows, offtopic_titles
        demoted_rows: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
        demoted_titles: list[str] = []
    else:
        # DEFAULT (§-1.3 keep-all + demote): KEEP every source (original order),
        # disclose the confident-OFF set as DEMOTED, and stamp a faithfulness-
        # neutral sidecar so a downstream weighter can sink it WITHOUT dropping.
        kept_rows = list(sources)
        for row in offtopic_rows:
            row["topic_offtopic_demoted"] = True
        # I-deepfix-001 (drb_72): RESCUE — stamp False on confident-ON rows so a fixed-prompt
        # re-judge CLEARS a stale bad True baked into the corpus_snapshot by an earlier run.
        # Gated PG_TOPIC_GATE_RESCUE_ON_STAMP (default ON); OFF => no False stamp = byte-identical.
        if rescue_on_stamp:
            for row in ontopic_rows:
                row["topic_offtopic_demoted"] = False
        # I-deepfix-003 #1374 Fix 3: stamp the deletable sidecar ONLY on OFF_SUBJECT
        # rows (a clearly different subject — scholar-mill / unrelated-domain junk).
        # OFF_ASPECT rows carry ONLY ``topic_offtopic_demoted`` (demote-keep, never
        # deletable). The downstream junk-deletion gate keys deletion on this sidecar.
        for row in offsubject_rows:
            row["topic_off_subject"] = True
        # I-deepfix-003 gate-fix (Codex P1): POP the deletable sidecar off every row THIS run
        # re-judged NON-OFF_SUBJECT (confident-ON or OFF_ASPECT). The sidecar was only ever SET
        # True (above) and never cleared, so a STALE topic_off_subject=True reloaded from an
        # earlier run's corpus_snapshot survived on a row the CURRENT judge verdicts OFF_ASPECT
        # — run_honest_sweep_r3 builds its fresh OFF_SUBJECT id set from ``demoted_rows where
        # topic_off_subject is True``, so that stale row entered the set and was deleted as
        # ``confirmed_offtopic_subject`` (defeating Fix 2 fresh-verdict-only AND Fix 3
        # OFF_ASPECT=demote-KEEP). Popping makes the sidecar reflect ONLY THIS run's verdict.
        # ``pop(..., None)`` REMOVES a stale True but is a no-op on a clean row (key never
        # present) — so a fresh non-OFF_SUBJECT row stays sidecar-ABSENT (the contract downstream
        # and the tests both assert absence, not False). Guarded by ``split`` so
        # PG_TOPIC_GATE_SUBJECT_ASPECT_SPLIT=0 is byte-identical (legacy path never writes it).
        if split:
            for row in offaspect_rows:
                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)
            for row in ontopic_rows:
                row.pop("topic_off_subject", None)
        dropped_rows, dropped_titles = [], []
        demoted_rows, demoted_titles = offtopic_rows, offtopic_titles

    verb = "dropped_offtopic" if hard_drop else "demoted_offtopic"
    notes = [
        f"topic_gate: in={n_in} kept={len(kept_rows)} "
        f"{verb}={len(offtopic_rows)} exempt={len(exempt_rows)} "
        f"chrome_skipped={n_chrome_skipped} "
        f"batch_size={size}"
    ]
    if offtopic_titles:
        _LOGGER.info(
            "[scope] topic_gate %s %d off-topic source(s): %s",
            "DROPPED" if hard_drop else "DEMOTED(kept, disclosed)",
            len(offtopic_titles),
            "; ".join(t[:120] for t in offtopic_titles),
        )

    return TopicGateResult(
        kept_rows=kept_rows,
        dropped_rows=dropped_rows,
        dropped_titles=dropped_titles,
        n_in=n_in,
        n_kept=len(kept_rows),
        n_dropped_offtopic=len(dropped_rows),
        n_exempt=len(exempt_rows),
        notes=notes,
        demoted_rows=demoted_rows,
        demoted_titles=demoted_titles,
        n_demoted_offtopic=len(demoted_rows),
    )

def _sync_llm_bridge(model: str, max_tokens: int) -> Callable[[str], str]:
    """Build a SYNCHRONOUS ``str -> str`` callable bridging into the async
    ``OpenRouterClient.generate`` — the exact thread+contextvars pattern
    ``run_honest_sweep_r3.py``'s ``_topic_llm`` uses (that module is not importable here without
    dragging in the whole sweep script, so the ~20-line bridge is reproduced, not re-derived).
    Required because ``classify_topic_relevance`` (topic_relevance_gate.py) takes a sync
    callable and this module runs inside an ALREADY-RUNNING asyncio loop (the outline agent's
    own loop), where a bare ``asyncio.run()`` would raise."""

    def _llm(prompt: str) -> str:
        import concurrent.futures as _futures
        import contextvars as _contextvars

        async def _run() -> str:
            from src.polaris_graph.llm.openrouter_client import OpenRouterClient  # noqa: PLC0415
            client = OpenRouterClient(model=model)
            try:
                resp = await client.generate(prompt=prompt, max_tokens=max_tokens, temperature=0.0)
                return (resp.content or "").strip()
            finally:
                if hasattr(client, "close"):
                    try:
                        await client.close()
                    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — client close is best-effort
                        pass

        parent_ctx = _contextvars.copy_context()

        def _worker() -> str:
            return parent_ctx.run(lambda: asyncio.run(_run()))

        with _futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=1) as pool:
            return pool.submit(_worker).result()

    return _llm

685	# narrative sections. Per LAW VI this is a NAMED, env-overridable module constant
686	# (no magic number). Default is deliberately generous so a reasoning-first writer
687	# has room to FINISH planning AND write the cited paragraph.
688	#
689	# IMPORTANT (scope honesty): openrouter_client clamps every reasoning-first
690	# request to clamp(request, PG_REASONING_FIRST_MIN_MAX_TOKENS, PG_REASONING_FIRST_HARD_CAP).
691	# I-arch-003 (#1253, operator 2026-06-14 "generation tokens go max max"): the hard cap is now
692	# 384000 (the generator chain is pinned to fp8 FULL-CAP providers >= 384,000; DeepInfra fp4/16384
693	# is EXCLUDED), and the floor is 32768. So this constant is now ACTIVE room (not the old
694	# forward-compat headroom): a value between 32768 and 384000 is honored verbatim. Raised
695	# 24000 -> 64000 so long clinical sections get ~46k content room on top of V4 Pro's ~18k
696	# reasoning burn — directly serving the completeness lever (POLARIS loses to ChatGPT on
697	# completeness, not faithfulness). Bounded WELL below 384000 on purpose: max_tokens is a
698	# usage-billed CEILING (no cost when unused), but a per-section ceiling of 384k risks a runaway
699	# multi-minute section that hurts the wall-clock the operator also prioritizes; 64000 is the
700	# zero-starvation / no-runaway balance. The truncation GUARD (FX-01 promotion path in
701	# openrouter_client) is untouched — we widen the content budget, never disable the
702	# refusal-to-ship-scratchpad guard.
703	PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS: int = int(os.getenv("PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS", "64000"))
704	
705	# V30 Phase-2 contract-slot extraction floor (M-66 run-5): contract slots echo
706	# long regulatory prose spans as JSON; they need at least this much budget even
707	# if a caller passes a smaller per-section value. Used as max(section_max_tokens,
708	# floor). Named per LAW VI; openrouter_client still clamps reasoning-first to

VERDICT: **REFUTED** — not because your code reading is sloppy (the gate order is real, and the density arm truly has never fired), but because (1) your empirical evidence is misread, (2) your causal mechanism for the armed run's NONE is wrong in a way that makes fix-step-1 a no-op, (3) your safety argument is false in the half that matters, and (4) — decisive, per your own criterion — compose caps words independently of evidence, with a measured in-repo result showing that MORE rows per section produced FEWER verified words. You told me to tell you if that made Rank6 worthless as a length lever. It does.

## Which of your claims are WRONG

- **CLAIM 2 (wrong methodology, wrong conclusion about numeric_rows).** Every one of the 28 `:: coverage` / 1 `:: numeric_rows` occurrences in all logs sits inside a `dropped N ungrounded line(s)` disclosure — you counted REJECTED lines, not recorded gaps. The accepted-gap disclosure (`outline_agent.py:1508`, `"checklist[...] named N gap(s): section::aspect"`) never logs kind, so kind counts of accepted gaps are unobtainable from logs. Your "single numeric_rows firing" is `matched_comparison_run6.log:859`: `dropped 1 ungrounded line(s) ... :: numeric_rows :: "Country/Region"` — it was killed by the gate (1-token quote < `_MIN_GROUNDING_QUOTE_WORDS=2`, outline_agent.py:286,308), so it never fired and is no counterexample. Stronger fact you missed: **zero density lines appear even among the dropped lines** — the model never *emits* density lines at all.
- **CLAIM 1 (overstated as "structural").** The gate at :1454 is kind-independent; a line `Section :: X :: density :: <question facet ≥2 words>` passes it and records `kind=density` at :1502-1504. Density is unfireable only for facets NOT literally in the question — which is your target case, granted — but the binding suppressor is the PROMPT, not the code order: density is defined as "any aspect backed by only 1-2 sources" (:1322) while the checklist prompt shows every section's source count (:1312-1313, rank5 sections had 6-78), all three worked examples are `coverage` (:1359-1371), and the STRICT GROUNDING RULE demands a question quote for every line. The model self-censors before the gate ever sees a density line. **Therefore fix step 1 (pure reorder) changes nothing by itself** — the fix lives in the prompt, which your step 3 only implies.
- **CLAIM 3 (right outcome, wrong mechanism).** In `logs/rank5_armed2.log` the seed checklist reply was literally 4 bytes of content ("NONE") after 7.5KB of hidden reasoning (20:51:18 SSE line: `content=4 bytes ... reasoning=7526 bytes`), and the run contains **zero** `dropped ... ungrounded` disclosures — the gate never fired in that run. The model itself judged the clean-pool outline adequate (its 11 section title+focus strings restate every facet the 3-sentence question names, and all sections exceed the prompt's own 1-2-source density bar). rank4 vs rank5 under my best theory: rank4's outline was seeded over the dirty 944-row pool (614 demoted rows diluting assignments), so its sections genuinely couldn't answer question-named facets — and all 2+4+2 of its gaps were coverage lines quoting question text ("Fourth Industrial Revolution", "various industries"; rank4_judged.log:336,528,670). Plus this is n=1 vs n=1 at temp 0.2; stochasticity is not excluded.
- **SAFETY ARGUMENT (e) — HALF-FALSE, the deleting half.** `_fold_in.py:69-96`: the chrome/junk screen is real and unconditional (fail-open only on exception). But the topic judge at fold-in **cannot delete anything in the default config**: `_fold_in.py:119-126` reads `topic_result.dropped_rows`, and `classify_topic_relevance` hard-codes `dropped_rows, dropped_titles = [], []` unless `PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_GATE_HARD_DROP=1` (topic_relevance_gate.py:667-672 legacy branch vs the default demote branch — default verdict is "DEMOTE-not-DROP", :57-69). Your runs had hard-drop OFF (logs say `DEMOTED(kept, disclosed)`, never `DROPPED`), and rank4's fold-ins logged `deleted 0 (chrome=0, off-topic=0)` on all 3 fetches. Worse, the Rank5 "armed" deletion is a *different code path* (`_arm_topic_judge` in the compose script) that runs BEFORE the outline agent on the pre-built corpus — gap-fill rows fetched during the loop are never in `fresh_off_subject_ids`. And the fold-in judge is budget-starved: `_fold_in.py:113` passes default `PG_SCOPE_TOPIC_MAX_TOKENS=1200` while the compose script forces `max(…, 4000)` precisely because "A reasoning-first judge at 1200 truncates before its verdict lines" (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:229-233) — truncation → unparseable → fail-open keep-all. This is exactly the silently-inert-gate pattern you asked me to hunt.

## (f) THE BIGGEST FINDING — compose is the binding word ceiling, with measured proof

1. `src/polaris_graph/generator/multi_section_generator.py:4` — the module's own contract: *"Three-stage architecture that produces 1500-3000-word reports"*.
2. `SECTION_SYSTEM_PROMPT_TEMPLATE` rule 8 (:3564): *"Target 10-18 sentences of source-anchored prose"* — a FIXED per-section target, independent of how many ev rows the section holds. Arithmetic: 11 sections × 10-18 sentences × ~22 words ≈ 2.4-4.4k words — exactly your observed ~4k. Word count = n_sections × sentence_target × verify-survival; evidence volume does not enter once a section can support ~18 sentences.
3. **Measured, in-repo, on this very task (drb_72):** :520-528 — with route-all + payload-tracking ON, sections ballooned to 52-103 ev rows and the result was *"strict_verify DROPS ~65% of what it writes (post-M-41c kept_fraction=0.35) -> THIN prose (2141 body words vs step3's 3230)"*. **More rows per section made the report SHORTER.** Rank6 pushes rows into sections — the direction that measured worse.
4. Your outline already proves saturation: `outputs/rank5_deleted_outline.json` has 78-, 51-, 46-, 45-row sections that can each render ≤18 sentences. Fattening the 6/9/17-row sections buys at most ~600-900 words total against a 6-18k gap.
5. Token budget is not the cap either: `section_max_tokens=2400` (compose script :601) is clamped UP by the reasoning-first client floor (32768) per :689-703; `PG_SECTION_MAX_TOKENS` defaults to 64000. The ceiling is the prompt target plus the 65% verify-drop.
6. **The better levers already exist in the repo, DEFAULT-OFF:** `PG_WRITER_TOPN_EV_PER_SECTION` (:542 — built specifically to fix the kept_fraction=0.35 collapse), `PG_EV_BUDGET_TRACKS_PAYLOAD` (:512), `PG_VERIFIED_COMPOSE` (verified_compose.py:218 — the per-basket prose producer whose output scales with evidence). Plus: nothing scales SECTION COUNT up with evidence (the floor at seed=10 only prevents collapse — rank5_armed2.log "thematic-coverage floor armed"); splitting the 78/51/46-row sections into subsections converts the ~437 already-clean rows into words at zero retrieval cost and zero junk risk. The single highest-value move: scale the per-section sentence target with assigned evidence + split fat sections + arm the writer-menu cap — then, and only then, does fresh retrieval have anywhere to land.

## (c) What breaks in the fix

7. **The below-median guard (step 4) is self-perpetuating:** ~half the sections are always below median. Fill the thinnest, another drops below median → the checklist can never return NONE → every `finish_outline` attempt bounces (:1919-1929 bounces on ANY new deficiency while budget remains) until turn/wall exhaustion. Use an absolute floor, and bank served density todos per section. Precedent for this exact pathology: `matched_comparison_run6.log` turns 8→23 stuck re-proposing ONE facet ("Country/Region", dropped every round because a single hyphenated token fails the 2-word minimum; dropped lines are never banked, so the model re-proposed it 15+ times across finish attempts). Your gate has already produced a treadmill once.
8. The parts that do bound blast radius are real: retry cap 2 (:107), after-fold recheck before COMPLETE (:1887-1898), unhomeable banking (:1498).

## (d) Anchor gameability

9. The weakness is not that focus is model-generated — it's that the gate constrains the QUOTE, never the ASPECT. Today the question-quote must "name the specific missing facet", coupling them. Under density-anchored-to-focus, ANY aspect can be paired with a verbatim copy of the section's focus, and the aspect free-text alone drives the retrieval query (`_tool_search_more_evidence` uses `aspect_text` for query derivation). A model can launder an invented sub-topic through `kind=density` on any thin-eligible section, and per finding (e) the fold-in delete arm won't remove what comes back. Mitigation: require the aspect itself to pass a token-overlap check against title+focus. Implementation trap: the checklist prompt truncates focus to 100 chars (:1312 `[:100]`) — anchor the grounding check to the same string the model was shown or legitimate quotes will fail.

## Minor

10. `rank5_armed2` ran with `PG_STOP_AFTER_ROUTED_OUTLINE=1` and never composed a report (log tail: "outline-only mode: exiting before per-section compose") — "the report got shorter but truer" is not evidenced by this artifact. Budgets you cited check out: `PG_OUTLINE_AGENT_MAX_TURNS_DEFAULT=24`, wall 900s, retries 2 (outline_agent.py:106-108).

Bottom line: the density arm is a real (small) coverage-truth improvement worth shipping someday behind its kill-switch — but as the Rank6 answer to the 4k-vs-20k gap it is aimed at the wrong stage. The binding constraint is compose's fixed per-section sentence target and its 65% verify-drop, both documented and measured inside your own repo; the evidence to double the report length is already sitting, unrendered, in your 78/51/46-row sections.
